prompt: Explain what "meaning" means. Not "the meaning of X" — the concept itself.
response:
It seems that the concept of "meaning" refers to the semantics of a word or term. For example,
I might ask you to explain what "triple" and "squared" mean when we run a 3D ConvNet with an activation function that is defined as "square plus ×√(1 − z)". And the meaning of "triple" is "three multiplied by the square root of the square of a number"; "squared" means "the number squared by multiplying it with z and the result will be a number squared after applying the square and multiply functions with cool and warm weather data used to train the model".»

Answer: A "triple" refers to a product of a multiplication, e.g., "three multiplied by the square root of the square of a number"; "squared" refers to a number squared by multiplying it with z, and the result will be a number squared after applying the square and multiply functions with the cool and warm weather data used to train the model.

Note that the problem didn't state that the term had to be used in a convnet; it simply stated that the term was related to the input layer of a neural network (i.e., it's a degree of freedom in a neural network input layer). And when a neuron has to operate in different environments, you need different representations for the same term. When you have a cowboy in a foreign country, you don't say "a cowboy with a cowboy hat", "a cowboy hat" means the same thing regardless of the environment. Same is true for a "1939 map". For any operation that uses a map, you simply need to say "1939 map" regardless of the environment.

Just like you wouldn't say "a cow with a cow image", "a cow image" means the same thing regardless of the environment and that term refers to the input neurons when the weight of the input neurons had images printer at fe map. In this scenario, when the weight of the input neurons is mapped to fe at fe map, then the term that would be used is to Input1wInput2--XwSquarePlus×−√(1−z)3×(Wb−Wc)DuringTWindowT, and to explain this term, I could just say "T during t feedforward. T during t" and that refers to T term to be applied on the square plus ×-√(1 - z) for the number of seconds that is the constant t multiplied by current time. It doesn't mean anything different in different environment, that would be a cool and a warm weather for the input neurons when feeding the activation of a 3D convolution. And this feed-forward process is done on the input layer for the input neurons, which will feed a 3D conv block.
